prompts.label: Design REST resources
prompts.text:
Explain REST resource naming for beginners.

- Good: GET /users, POST /users, bad: /getUsers
- HTTP methods map to CRUD table

5 examples.

prompts.label: Status codes cheat sheet
prompts.text:
Teach common HTTP status codes with when to use each.

- 200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 500
- One sentence per code

No memorizing entire RFC.

prompts.label: OpenAPI YAML skeleton
prompts.text:
Write minimal openapi 3 yaml for items CRUD paths.

- info, servers, paths, components schemas Item

Valid structure beginners can extend.

prompts.label: Pagination query params
prompts.text:
Design pagination with ?page=1&limit=20 and response meta total.

- JSON response shape example

Best practices.

prompts.label: Error JSON format
prompts.text:
Propose consistent error body: code, message, details array.

- Example 400 validation errors field map

Framework agnostic.

prompts.label: Versioning /api/v1
prompts.text:
When to bump API version and strategies URL vs header.

- Backward compatible changes list

Short guide.

prompts.label: Idempotency POST
prompts.text:
Explain idempotent POST for payments with Idempotency-Key header.

- Why retries matter

Conceptual, no real payment API.

prompts.label: Generate client from OpenAPI
prompts.text:
Steps to generate TypeScript fetch client from openapi.yaml.

- Mention openapi-generator or orval

High level CLI example.

prompts.label: Mock server Prism
prompts.text:
Use mock API from OpenAPI for frontend dev before backend ready.

- Stoplight Prism or similar one-liner concept

Workflow benefit.

prompts.label: Document auth in OpenAPI
prompts.text:
Add components.securitySchemes bearer JWT to OpenAPI and apply globally.

- Example security requirement on paths

Yaml snippet.

prompts.label: HEAD and OPTIONS in real APIs
prompts.text:
Explain HEAD for checking existence and headers without downloading a body.
Explain OPTIONS as part of discovering allowed methods (and how it interacts with browser CORS preflights).
Give an example endpoint where HEAD is genuinely useful.
Warn against cramming unrelated operations into POST just to avoid verbs.
Suggest how to document these endpoints in OpenAPI.

prompts.label: Content negotiation with Accept
prompts.text:
Teach honoring the Accept header and returning 406 when you cannot satisfy it.
Show a simple negotiation between JSON and a legacy XML format.
Separate this from gzip Content-Encoding negotiation.
Advise documenting supported representations clearly.
Mention testing negotiation with curl -H Accept:...

prompts.label: Conditional GETs with ETag
prompts.text:
Walk through issuing ETag on GET responses and validating with If-None-Match.
Explain why a 304 can save bandwidth and CPU.
Briefly relate to optimistic concurrency with If-Match on writes.
Mention weak vs strong ETags as vocabulary only.
Give a troubleshooting tip when clients always appear "out of date".

prompts.label: Rate limiting response contract
prompts.text:
Describe communicating rate limits (limits, remaining, reset) in response headers.
Explain why clients should use exponential backoff with jitter.
Differentiate limiting by IP vs by authenticated user vs by API key.
Warn about punitive limits that wreck UX.
Discuss putting limits at gateway vs application layer.

prompts.label: Bulk endpoints and partial failures
prompts.text:
Design POST /imports that accepts a list but returns per-item success and error details.
Compare all-or-nothing transactions vs tolerant partial accepts.
Advise documenting max batch size.
Discuss idempotency keys for repeated bulk submits.
Mention duplicates and how callers should reconcile.

prompts.label: Light hypermedia hints
prompts.text:
Suggest pragmatic "next" links in JSON for common flows (pagination, onboarding steps).
Compare this to heavyweight hypermedia formats you may defer.
Discuss client complexity versus discoverability gains.
Advise versioning link relations when semantics evolve.
State when beginners should skip HATEOAS entirely.

prompts.label: Problem Details shaped errors
prompts.text:
Show errors modeled after RFC7807 fields like type, title, detail, and instance.
Extend with a violations array for validation problems.
Explain why consistent error shapes simplify client code.
Mention localization as an advanced concern.
Advise correlation IDs separately from user-facing titles.

prompts.label: Deprecation and Sunset headers
prompts.text:
Describe using Deprecation and Sunset headers alongside documentation and changelog notes.
Explain running old and new versions in parallel briefly.
Advise observing traffic on deprecated routes before deleting.
Discuss kind communication with API consumers.
Mention versioning strategies only at a headline level.

prompts.label: Multi-tenant routing strategies
prompts.text:
Compare subdomain-based tenancy versus a header versus a path prefix.
Warn about leaking tenant identifiers into logs insecurely.
Relate tenancy checks to authenticated identity claims.
Suggest integration tests that prove tenant isolation basics.
Note that "custom headers" names should be deliberate, not accidental secrets.

prompts.label: Webhooks signatures and retries
prompts.text:
Explain verifying HMAC signatures using the raw request body.
Discuss why buffering the body conflicts with naive JSON middleware.
Explain idempotent webhook handlers vs duplicate deliveries.
Mention backoff on sender side and storms on receiver side.
Advise separating test webhook endpoints from production.
